title: Count length-6 binary strings with no 3-in-a-row
prompt: Consider strings of length 6 over the two-letter alphabet {H, T}.
Count how many such strings contain NO run of three or more identical characters
in a row -- that is, strings in which neither 'HHH' nor 'TTT' appears as a
Output ONLY the single integer between <answer> and </answer> tags.
difficulty_rationale: A counting problem that rewards a structured method -- a DP / recurrence over run lengths, or the runs-as-compositions insight giving 2*Fibonacci -- over brute enumeration of 64 cases. Easy to get subtly wrong by mis-handling the run-length-2 boundary. Answer brute-force-verified: 26. Expected to separate careful high-effort reasoning from quick low-effort guesses.
